---
title: "Global Setup for Viral Sequence Analysis"
author: "Your Name"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
source(file.path(dirname(knitr::current_input()), "globalSetup.R"))
```

# Shared Setup Overview

This document loads the shared analysis setup used by the DNA sequence report.
It validates package availability, sets the knitting root directory, and checks
that the FASTA files exist in `classwork/assets`.

# Package Status

```{r package-status}
data.frame(
  Package = c(required_packages, names(optional_package_status)),
  Required = c(rep(TRUE, length(required_packages)), rep(FALSE, length(optional_package_status))),
  Available = c(
    rep(TRUE, length(required_packages)),
    unname(optional_package_status)
  )
)
```

# Load Sequences

```{r load-data}
mexico <- read.fasta(file.path(assets_dir, "mexico.fasta"))
wuhan <- read.fasta(file.path(assets_dir, "wuhan.fasta"))
francia <- read.fasta(file.path(assets_dir, "francia.fasta"))
```

# Sequence Lengths

```{r lengths}
cat("Longitud Mexico:", length(mexico[[1]]), "\n")
cat("Longitud Wuhan:", length(wuhan[[1]]), "\n")
cat("Longitud Francia:", length(francia[[1]]), "\n")
```

# Base Composition Plots

```{r plots}
compara <- function(mex, wuh, fra) {
  par(mfrow = c(3, 1))

  bases <- c("a", "c", "g", "t")

  barplot(table(factor(mex, levels = bases)),
    col = 1:4, main = "Mexico"
  )

  barplot(table(factor(wuh, levels = bases)),
    col = 1:4, main = "Wuhan"
  )

  barplot(table(factor(fra, levels = bases)),
    col = 1:4, main = "Francia"
  )
}

compara(mexico[[1]], wuhan[[1]], francia[[1]])
```

# Session Info (Debugging)

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
